questions.question: Which HTML tag is used to define a paragraph?
questions.explanation:
The

tag defines a paragraph of text. Browsers automatically add margin above and below paragraphs to visually separate them.

questions.question: How does HTML handle multiple consecutive spaces in paragraph text?
choices: Multiple spaces collapse into a single space
questions.explanation: HTML collapses multiple whitespace characters (spaces, tabs, newlines) into a single space. To preserve whitespace, use the <pre> tag or CSS white-space property.

questions.question:
What does the
tag do?
choices: Inserts a line break within text
questions.explanation:
The
tag inserts a single line break. Unlike

, it does not create a new block-level element with margins. It is a void element and has no closing tag.

questions.question: Which tag preserves whitespace and displays text in a fixed-width font?
questions.explanation: The <pre> (preformatted) tag preserves all whitespace including spaces, tabs, and line breaks. It renders text in a monospace font, making it useful for code or ASCII art.

questions.question:
The
tag is a ___ element, meaning it has no closing tag.
questions.explanation:
Void elements like
, , and
cannot have content and do not need a closing tag. In HTML5, the trailing slash (
) is optional.

questions.question: The HTML entity ___ is used to insert a non-breaking space that will not be collapsed.
questions.explanation: The   entity inserts a space that browsers will not collapse. It also prevents a line break from occurring at that position in the text.
